note: Primary inbound object; Objects.Sender ships alongside. Consumed as a mix dep (loader :verify attestation) — the files list for :require-mode loading is deliberately omitted until an IR-cast swarm needs it. config_schema covers the Ingress; a Sender schema can follow the same pattern.

bot_token.description: Literal bot token (tests/legacy only — prefer bot_token_env).

bot_token_env.description: NAME of the env var holding the bot token; resolved by the object at init, never stored in config.

bot_ref.description: Non-secret bot namespace (default: fingerprint of the token).

bot_username.description: Bot @username for group-mention addressing (env fallback GENSWARMS_TELEGRAM_BOT_USERNAME).

fail_open_without_username?.description: Whether group addressing works without a known username (fail-closed by default).

session_opts.description: Session runtime options (slot pool, backend, skills, llm proxy…).

binding_authority.description: Node authoritative for slot bindings (default telegram_ingress).

poll_enabled.description: getUpdates long-polling on/off (single consumer per token).

allowed_updates.description: Telegram update types requested from getUpdates.

poll_health_sink.description: Optional 1-arity fun invoked after each poll result fold with %{last_poll_ok_ms:, conflict_count:, poll_failures:, at_ms:} (programmatic config only — never wire-settable, hence no JSON type). Wrapped total: a raising sink never breaks the poll loop.
